last_report:
### CONFIRMED — Charlotte Hornets ML

- Match: **Orlando Magic vs Charlotte Hornets** | Tip: 2026-04-17T23:30Z
- Pick: Charlotte Hornets ML | Scout odds: 1.68 | Commit odds: **1.68** | Move: **0.00 (no movement)**
- ML: ORL 2.35 / CHA 1.68 | ATS: ORL +3.5 (1.92/2.00) | O/U: 218.5 (O:1.96/U:1.96)
- Stake: **€374.56** | Pot: **€629.26** | Conf: **75/100** | EV: **0.26 (26%)**
- Decision: Confirmed with increased confidence (70 → 75). Key update: Jonathan Isaac upgraded from Questionable to **OUT** (confirmed NBA official PDF — Left Knee Sprain). Isaac was ORL's primary rim protector; his confirmed absence removes the -5 conservative Scout discount and strengthens CHA's path to victory. Core thesis unchanged: CHA L15 NetRtg +25.7 vs ORL -6.7 (32.4-point gap), superior season NetRtg (+5.0 vs +0.6), OffRtg advantage (119.5 vs 115.1). ORL home court partially offsets but cannot overcome this talent/form gap with Isaac now officially OUT.

---

### CONFIRMED — Phoenix Suns ML

- Match: **Phoenix Suns vs Golden State Warriors** | Tip: 2026-04-18T02:00Z
- Pick: Phoenix Suns ML | Scout odds: 1.68 | Commit odds: **1.71** | Move: **+0.03 (improved in our favour)**
- ML: PHX 1.71 / GSW 2.30 | ATS: PHX -2.5 (1.92/2.00) | O/U: 220.5 (O:2.04/U:1.88)
- Stake: **€280.92** | Pot: **€480.37** | Conf: **68/100** | EV: **0.163 (16.3%)**
- Decision: Confirmed. Odds drifted +0.03 in our favour (1.68 → 1.71) — noted, confirmed at original stake tier per line movement rules (do not increase stake on favourable drift). No new injuries — Jimmy Butler III remains OUT, PHX anchors (Booker, Durant, Beal) all healthy. Thesis intact: PHX L15 +6.7 vs GSW -16.8 (23.5-point gap), GSW L3 streak and 3-7 L10, PHX home court advantage (+3pts play-in). EV improved to 16.3% at commit odds.

---

### LATE SCOUT SUMMARY
All 6 non-play-in games checked — no new bets added. Four games (CLE, DEN, NYK, BOS as favourites) have ML odds far below the 1.65 floor; corresponding underdogs are above the 2.50 ceiling. All spread picks from these games were declined due to absence of injury data in tonight's nba_official feed (which only covers the 4 play-in teams). One available late pick slot was preserved but no qualifying edge found.

---

### SLATE SUMMARY
Both play-in draft picks confirmed with strong conviction. Total committed: **€655.48 (35.0% of €1,872.81 bankroll)** — well within the 70% maximum. The slate's strongest signal remains the historic CHA L15 advantage (+25.7 vs ORL's -6.7) now bolstered by Isaac's confirmed OUT status; PHX vs GSW offers a clean directional bet against a depleted, poor-form Warriors side in a home elimination game scenario. No late picks added due to odds range violations and incomplete injury data on non-play-in games.

settled_bets.match: Atlanta Hawks vs New York Knicks
settled_bets.reasoning: COMMIT REVIEW — CONFIRMED. Scout odds 2.02, commit odds 2.00 (movement -0.02, minimal, within 0.10 threshold). Anchor players Brunson, KAT, Bridges all active (not listed on injury report). ATL injury update: only RayJ Dennis Questionable (G League Two-Way — irrelevant to thesis). Core thesis intact: NYK season NetRtg +6.6 vs ATL +2.6 (4.0-pt gap in NYK's favour). ATL L10 hot streak fade signal valid (8-2 L10 but 45-33 season record vs NYK 50-28). NYK better season record, better NetRtg, healthy franchise players. ATL's surge (L15 +36.6) flagged but regression-fade at 2.00 still strong value. EV recalculated at commit odds: EV = (0.72 × 2.00) - 1 = 0.44. Well above 0.05 threshold. Odds 2.00 within ML range (1.65-2.50). Confidence 72 (High tier) unchanged. Stake maintained at €150 (15% — conservative within High tier due to ATL home court and L15 surge counter-signal).

settled_bets.match: Denver Nuggets vs Portland Trail Blazers
settled_bets.reasoning: COMMIT REVIEW — CONFIRMED at current line. Scout drafted DEN -8.5 at 1.94. Current market: DEN -8.0 at 1.95. Spread compressed 0.5 pts (slight adjustment against DEN spread), but odds improved +0.01 to 1.95. Anchor check: Jokic, Murray, MPJ all active (none listed on injury report). POR injury update confirms thesis: Damian Lillard OUT (injury management confirmed by NBA official), Jerami Grant OUT (right calf), Shaedon Sharpe OUT (reaction), Vit Krejci OUT. POR missing four key contributors including franchise player Lillard. DEN core intact with Jokic (presumed available), home court advantage at Ball Arena, W8 streak, 9-1 L10. Season NetRtg gap: DEN +4.8 vs POR -0.4 = 5.2 pts in DEN's favour. Counter-signal noted: POR L15 +46.2 is anomalously high but roster tonight is severely depleted — L15 figure does not reflect tonight's deployment. At -8.0 (easier cover than -8.5), the pick retains strong value. EV = (0.68 × 1.95) - 1 = 0.326. Spread odds 1.95 within target (1.80-2.30). Confidence 68 (Medium tier) unchanged. Stake €150 maintained.

LATE PICK — ADDED AT COMMIT. Scout noted this game and drafted SAS -8.5 at 1.90 but it was not included in final draft picks for commit review. Re-evaluated at commit time. TIME GATE: Game at 00:00 UTC, commit at 22:27 UTC = 93 min > 20 min ✅. ODDS GATE: SAS -8.5 at 1.89. Within spread target 1.80-2.30 ✅. QUANTITY GATE: First and only new pick at commit phase ✅. LINE MOVEMENT: Scout had 1.90, now 1.89 — movement of -0.01, negligible. INJURY CHECK (NBA Official): SAS injury report shows only Harrison Ingram Out (G League), David Jones Garcia Out (ankle surgery), Emanuel Miller Out (G League) — no franchise-level absences. PHI: Johni Broome Out (recovery), Cameron Payne Out (strain) — no franchise-level absences. SAS anchor players (Wembanyama, Castle, Vassell) not listed = active. THESIS: SAS L15 +48.9 (elite, top of entire league). Season NetRtg +8.2 vs PHI -0.2 = 8.4-pt gap. SAS record 59-19 vs PHI 43-35. SAS L10 9-1 vs PHI 6-4. SAS home at Frost Bank Center. PHI L15 +11.9 is decent but SAS form is genuinely elite — record validates this is not a misleading hot streak on a mediocre team (59-19 is a top-tier record). CONFIDENCE: Base 65 (SAS clearly superior team, home, elite form) + 5 (PHI depleted: Broome, Payne out) = 70. EV = (0.70 × 1.89) - 1 = 1.323 - 1 = 0.323 ≥ 0.05 ✅. STAKE: Confidence 70 = High tier (70-84) = 20-25% of €1,000. Use 20% = €200. Total committed stake after all picks: €150 + €150 + €200 = €500 = 50% of bankroll ≤ 70% ✅.

settled_bets.match: Los Angeles Lakers vs Oklahoma City Thunder
settled_bets.reasoning: Late-breaking trigger: LeBron James confirmed OUT (Management) at commit time — was Questionable/GTD at Scout. Combined with Luka Doncic OUT, Austin Reaves OUT, Marcus Smart OUT, the Lakers are playing with a skeleton roster of reserves. OKC (62-16) has SGA healthy and a full squad minus Jalen Williams — still elite. OKC L15 NetRtg: +48.0 vs LAL L15: +3.8 — gap of +44.2. Line moved from +16.5 to +18.5 as LeBron OUT was confirmed; movement is fully explained by the injury news. OKC -18.5 at 1.92 represents genuine value against a decimated opponent. EV = (0.62 × 1.92) - 1 = 0.1904.

LATE SCOUT PICK — NEW EDGE SINCE 14:00. STEP 0 — LINE ANOMALY CHECK: PHX ML 1.16 (outside range), DAL ML 6.39 (outside range). PHX spread -12.5 at 1.99 is within valid spread range (1.75-2.30). No anomaly on spread line. STEP 1 — INJURY TRIGGER: Since Scout, official injury report confirms DAL missing: Kyrie Irving OUT (Left Knee Surgery — franchise player), Dereck Lively II OUT (Right Foot Surgery — key big), plus Klay Thompson Doubtful (Rest), Caleb Martin Doubtful (Strain), Naji Marshall Doubtful (Hip), Daniel Gafford Doubtful (Impingement). DAL is operating with a skeleton crew — essentially no reliable rotation. STEP 2 — NETRTG ANALYSIS: PHX L15 +8.6 vs DAL L15 -29.3 — gap of 37.9 points. Season NetRtg: PHX +1.4 vs DAL -5.6. PHX trending upward; DAL in a steep decline with a depleted roster. STEP 3 — FORM: PHX L10 4-6 is a cold stretch concern (Scout previously flagged this), but the absence of virtually all DAL key players overrides form concerns for spread purposes. DAL L10 2-8. STEP 4 — PHX KEY PLAYERS: Kevin Durant and Devin Booker NOT on injury report (active). Grayson Allen Questionable (Contusion — role player, not franchise, standard -5% stake adjustment not required). Mark Williams OUT and Highsmith OUT (Management — not key to PHX spread). PHX core intact. STEP 5 — SPREAD EVALUATION: PHX -12.5 at home. With DAL missing Kyrie (their best player) and multiple other key contributors Doubtful/Out, a 12.5-pt home win is achievable for PHX. PHX season NetRtg advantage (+1.4 vs -5.6 = 7.0 pts season), L15 gap (+37.9 pts), and home court (~3 pts) support the spread. STEP 6 — CONFIDENCE: Base 65 (strong matchup edge with depleted DAL), -3 for PHX L10 cold form concern = 62. EV = (0.62 × 1.99) - 1 = 1.2338 - 1 = 0.234. STEP 7 — ODDS VALIDATION: 1.99 within spread range (1.75-2.30). EV 0.234 >> 0.05 threshold. STEP 8 — STAKING: Confidence 62 = Medium tier (15-20%). 15% of €1,339 = €200.85, rounded to €200. Total exposure: 14.9% of bankroll — well within 70% limit. Late scout quantity gate: this is pick 1 of max 1 allowed.

CONFIRMED from draft nba_draft_20260409_003 with minor odds drift of -0.04 (2.0 → 1.96). Odds remain within spread target range 1.80–2.30 (above 1.75 commit floor). EV recalc at 1.96: (0.68 × 1.96) - 1 = 0.3328 = +33.3%, well above 0.05 minimum. Washington is 17-62, L10 1-9, confirmed tanking team. Official injury report confirms Washington missing Bilal Coulibaly OUT (Bursitis), Anthony Davis OUT (Left Finger Sprain), Kyshawn George OUT (Left Elbow Sprain). Note: Trae Young and Alex Sarr not listed in current official report — may be available. Even so, Washington is a full tank-tier team with extreme roster depletion. L15 NetRtg gap: WAS -39.5 vs CHI -32.8 = 6.7 point gap (above 6-point threshold per skills file spread rules). Chicago anchor players Coby White, Nikola Vucevic, Patrick Williams all confirmed available (not listed in injury report). Chicago missing Buzelis, Collins, Essengue, Giddey, Okoro, Richards — but still far superior roster vs Washington. Skills: Tank team as large underdog — tankers don't cover spreads. Spread confidence floor 60 met (68 ≥ 60). Stake: Medium tier (55-69) = 15% × €1139 = €171.

LATE SCOUT PICK — New information since 14:00 Scout: Joel Embiid confirmed OUT (Illness) per NBA official injury report. Embiid is Philadelphia's franchise player and primary offensive engine. This dramatically weakens PHI's ability to cover a -6.5 spread on the road. PHI also missing Johni Broome OUT (Recovery) and Cameron Payne OUT (Strain). Houston is home, 50-29, L10 8-2, L15 NetRtg +39.9 (elite). Houston anchors Jalen Green, Alperen Sengun, Dillon Brooks all available. PHI L15 +4.9 vs HOU L15 +39.9 — 35-point gap. PHI season NetRtg -0.4 (negative, regression candidate); without Embiid this worsens substantially. Houston 7-game win streak context. Houston DefRtg 113.3 vs PHI OffRtg 115.7 — PHI's offense collapses without Embiid. LINE MOVEMENT EXPLANATION: HOU ML moved 1.60 → 1.43 confirming books also priced in Embiid's absence; spread 1.98 represents value as market hasn't fully re-priced spread relative to ML move. ODDS VALIDATION: 1.98 within spread target 1.80–2.30 (above 1.75 commit floor) ✓. EV = (0.72 × 1.98) - 1 = 1.4256 - 1 = 0.4256 = +42.6% ✓. Confidence 72 ≥ 60 gate ✓. Time gate: 00:00Z is ~86 min away (> 20 min) ✓. Quantity gate: this is the 1 allowed new pick at Commit phase ✓. Stake: High tier (70-84) = 20% × €1139 = €227.80 → €228.

Late scout pick. HOU (51-29, L10: 8-2, W8 streak, NetRtg L15: +39.0) is dominant at home vs MIN (47-33, L10: 4-6, NetRtg L15: -4.6). NetRtg gap of 43.6 points in HOU's favour is the primary signal. HOU on an 8-game winning streak with elite home form. MIN's Anthony Edwards listed Questionable (Maintenance) — franchise player rules applied: confidence -10, stake -20%. Edge exists regardless of Edwards presence given the enormous L15 form gap. HOU missing Adams (OUT) and VanVleet (OUT) but these were factored into the base confidence. Spread of -8.5 at 1.85 is within valid range. EV = 0.11 at adjusted confidence 60. Injury source nba_official, no data quality concerns.

settled_bets.match: Minnesota Timberwolves vs New Orleans Pelicans
settled_bets.reasoning: Late-breaking edge: NBA official injury report confirms MIN franchise players Anthony Edwards and Rudy Gobert are NOT listed on the injury report and are active tonight, reversing Scout's assessment that MIN was severely depleted. Scout rejected MIN due to reported absences of Edwards, Gobert, Randle — these players are not listed OUT at commit time, indicating they are available. NOP is a confirmed tank-tier team (26-55, L10 1-9, L15 -31.0) with 6 players OUT including Murphy, Missi, Bey, Jones, Matkovic, McGowens. MIN at home with core intact vs a gutted NOP roster at only -5.5 represents strong value — the line appears not to have fully adjusted to MIN's returning core. MIN 48-33, home court advantage, and the talent gap with NOP far exceeds a 5.5-point spread.

PLAY-IN GAME — PHI (#7) vs ORL (#8) at home. Primary edge: L15 NetRtg gap of +13.2 (PHI +9.1 vs ORL -4.1) — dominant signal in PHI's favour. Home court in play-in context adds further edge (~+3 pts). Odds moved marginally in favour (+0.01: 1.84→1.85). Anchor player adjustments at commit: Jett Howard NOT on injury report (Active) — one less ORL injury concern. Jonathan Isaac still Questionable (Left Knee Sprain); thesis does not depend on his absence but applies standard -10 confidence adjustment and -20% stake per rules. Confidence adjusted 68→58. Embiid OUT was already priced in and team-adjusted over extended absence. EV at commit: (0.58 × 1.85) - 1 = 0.073 ≥ 0.05. Stake adjusted to 80% of 15% tier due to Questionable player: 15% × €1535.60 × 0.80 = €184.27.

picks.reasoning: LINE ANOMALY CHECK: No anomaly — CHA priced as favourite at 1.68 with better record and superior stats, consistent with expectations. PRIORITY STATS: (1) NetRtg L15 GAP: CHA L15 +25.7 vs ORL L15 -6.7 — gap of 32.4 points, massive signal in Charlotte's favour. This is the dominant signal. (2) No B2B issues — both teams have standard rest in play-in context. (3) FRANCHISE PLAYER INJURIES: ORL has Jonathan Isaac Questionable (Left Knee) — Isaac is a key defensive piece but not a franchise player; confidence -5 applied. CHA has Moussa Diabate Questionable (Left Hip) — bench depth player, minimal impact; no confidence adjustment needed. (4) MOTIVATION: Both teams are in play-in, fully motivated. CHA as #8 seed faces the 7v8 loser if they lose; ORL as #7 seed (from context: ORL is the 7-seed based on 45-37 record vs CHA 44-38) — home court advantage applies here for ORL, worth ~3pts in play-in per rules. (5) L10: CHA 6-4 vs ORL 7-3 — ORL has slight L10 edge. (6) HOME COURT: ORL is the home team — adds ~3 pts of value in play-in per playoff_rest rules. Significant factor. (7) Season NetRtg: CHA +5.0 vs ORL +0.6 — CHA meaningfully superior. (8) DefRtg: Both teams at 114.5 — no gap; symmetric. OffRtg: CHA 119.5 vs ORL 115.1 — CHA clearly superior offensively. SYNTHESIS: The L15 gap of 32.4 is extraordinary — CHA has been in elite form, ORL has been poor. CHA's season NetRtg (+5.0 vs +0.6) and OffRtg advantage support the directional signal. ORL's home court (+3pts) and marginally better L10 are real but insufficient to overcome a 32-point L15 gap. Isaac Questionable (ORL) reduces their defensive anchor; apply -5 confidence for potential absence impact. Base confidence: 75 (strong L15 signal, NetRtg and OffRtg gap). Adjustments: Isaac Questionable on ORL side — this HELPS CHA bet, no penalty needed for CHA. For ORL home court value, reduce CHA confidence by 5 (home worth ~3pts in play-in). Net confidence: 70. EV = (0.70 × 1.68) - 1 = 1.176 - 1 = 0.176. EV = 17.6% — STRONG edge (≥ 0.15). Confidence 70 meets ML floor of 50. Odds 1.68 meets commit confirmation floor of 1.65 but is below ML target range of 1.60-2.50 — 1.68 IS within 1.60-2.50 range. Stake: confidence 70 = High tier (20-25% of bankroll) = 20% × €1872.81 = €374.56.

picks.match: Phoenix Suns vs Golden State Warriors
picks.reasoning: LINE ANOMALY CHECK: No anomaly — PHX priced as home favourite at 1.68, consistent with better record (45-37 vs 37-45) and home court. GSW priced at 2.35. PRIORITY STATS: (1) NetRtg L15 GAP: PHX L15 +6.7 vs GSW L15 -16.8 — gap of 23.5 points, well above the 4.0 threshold. Massive directional signal for PHX. (2) No B2B issues in play-in per playoff_rest rules. (3) FRANCHISE PLAYER INJURIES: GSW has Jimmy Butler III OUT (Right ACL — Surgery), Moses Moody OUT (Surgery), Quinten Post OUT (Management), and Kristaps Porzingis QUESTIONABLE (Right Ankle). Butler OUT is significant — he's a franchise-level contributor. PHX has Grayson Allen QUESTIONABLE (Left Hamstring) and Mark Williams QUESTIONABLE (Left Foot) — Allen is secondary player, Williams is a rotation center; combined confidence -5 applied. GSW missing Butler (OUT) = franchise-level absence per rules; cannot bet GSW ML. For PHX pick: opponent (GSW) missing Butler = supports PHX bet. Butler OUT for GSW means do not bet GSW to win — but this SUPPORTS PHX. Porzingis Questionable for GSW (additional -10 on any GSW bet, irrelevant here). PHX Questionable players: -5 combined. (4) MOTIVATION: Both teams are in play-in; 9-seed (GSW) faces maximum elimination pressure. PHX as #7 seed (45-37) vs GSW as #9 seed (37-45). 9-seed elimination desperation is real but GSW's poor form (L3 streak, negative NetRtg) makes desperation insufficient. (5) L10: PHX 5-5 vs GSW 3-7 — GSW L10 is notably poor. (6) HOME COURT: PHX is the home team — +3 pts in play-in per rules. Significant advantage. (7) Season NetRtg: PHX +1.4 vs GSW -0.6 — PHX ahead. (8) GSW FADE CANDIDATE: Per skills emerging_fade section — GSW at L3 streak, NetRtg negative, do not back without significant reason. (9) Pace: PHX 97.2, GSW 99.0 — slight mismatch (1.8 pts), below 5pt threshold for unpredictability concern. SYNTHESIS: L15 gap of 23.5 strongly favours PHX. Season stats, L10, home court, and opponent roster depletion (Butler OUT) all stack on PHX side. GSW's desperation as 9-seed partially offset by terrible recent form and franchise player absences. Base confidence: 75. Adjustments: PHX Questionable players (Allen + Williams) -5; PHX confirmation as 7-seed vs 9-seed play-in edge +3. Net confidence: 68 (rounding). Note: GSW 37-45 is below .550 W% — per HOT STREAK FADE RULE reversed: GSW on L3, not a hot team; PHX is the more stable team here. EV = (0.68 × 1.68) - 1 = 1.1424 - 1 = 0.1424. EV = 14.2% — marginal-to-strong edge (≥ 0.05, approaching 0.15). Confidence 68 meets ML floor of 50. Odds 1.68 within 1.60-2.50 range. Stake: confidence 68 = Medium tier (15-20%) = 15% × €1872.81 = €281.92.

scout_report_data.report: Two-game play-in slate with clear directional signals. Injury feed source is NBA Official (4 teams reporting) — full confidence rules apply. Two ML picks drafted: CHA ML driven by an extraordinary L15 NetRtg gap (+25.7 vs -6.7 = 32.4pt gap) against a struggling ORL squad despite home court disadvantage. PHX ML supported by L15 gap of 23.5 points over GSW, compounded by Butler/Moody OUT for Warriors and GSW's L3 streak/negative season NetRtg. Spreads evaluated for both games but ML edges are cleaner given play-in variance and injury question marks on marginal players. Pace data available but both games near threshold — O/U signals inconclusive; no totals drafted. Total stake: €656.48 (35.1% of bankroll, within 70% daily cap). 30%+ reserve maintained.
